title: Shikinami really isnt so bad
wikitext:
[]: Shikinami really isn't so bad             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Tue Aug 03, 2010 8:02 pm Disclaimer: I am in no way saying Shikinami is in any way shape or form better than Soryu. The original Asuka is the love of my life (next to my wife) and is probably my favorite character of any work of fiction ever. I know we had a thread like this in the past and it got locked (my fault if I recall), but after I watched 2.22 again, I have to say Asuka Shikinami isn't a bad character at all, she just got a bad wrap from fans. I say this because when I watched Eva 2.22 this time with two friends who hated Asuka before, they now love her. The problem was that as soon as reviews came in for 2.0, everyone was saying, "Asuka is now officially Tsundere." Which may or may not be true, but if you strip away all the bullshit jargon fans have come up with over the years to describe things is anime ("like the director is Trolling us!") you will realize that Asuka is now a little more sweeter than she was before because the audience had to feel bad for her a lot quicker. Lets be honest. If Soryu had been in 2.0, most casual people wouldn't give a shit that she got in Unit 03 and got creamed. They would say, "I hope the bitch is dead." We all like Soryu because we got to see her grow and develop over a longer period of time. Even her sweet moments were filled with hostility. If this film had to succeed for a more casual audience then Asuka had to be a lot more sympathetic, and by all accounts she was. My two friends I mentioned earlier who would give me endless amouts of shit every time I said Asuka Soryu is the greatest character in modern anime, actually felt sad for Shikinami after what happened for her. They felt nothing for Soryu during End of Evangelion. So again, I'm not saying she's better, or worse, she is just different, and she performs her job well within the context of the movie. Anno didn't go out and say, "I'm going to make her more Tsundere." In fact I'd be surprised if he even is aware of the term. A lot of these directors don't follow fan lingo or theorys at all and he seems like the kind of guy who could give a crap. (Yes I know Gainax is an Otaku studio that he started, but he is far far removed from the kid he was when he started Gainax). He probably just thought, "If I make Asuka a little nicer, then the audience will feel worse when she gets in Unit 03." And no that is not trolling either. That is him doing his job as director/writer. I think as fans we over think some things. From a strictly storyline standpoint, Asuka Shikinami is a good character. Standing next to Soryu, she doesn't compare, but that isn't what she is meant to do.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Tue Aug 03, 2010 8:21 pm I don't have a problem with Rebuild Asuka's personality per se; in fact, she's pretty much the only relatable human character in a movie populated by Martian nanobots. This isn't a point in the movie's favor, since Shinji is supposed to be the protagonist.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Aug 03, 2010 10:09 pm NGE is NGE. Rebuild is another thing, working towards different ends that won't become wholly apparent 'til the conclusion. I know it's REALLY hard for people like us, but Rebuild deserves to be judged apart from the baggage of NGE. Shikinami should be judged by how well she's Shikinami and not Soryu.

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Tue Aug 03, 2010 10:14 pm I think that where they go with her character in 3.0 will say a lot more than what we got in 2.0 but as it stands now... I feel slighted. They very clearly and deliberately play on fan expectations and flip them on you. The idea of surprising the old audience had them thinking what they presented in 2.0 was crushing and emotionally distressing but I don't think that it resonates any more clearly than what we got in the series. Asuka latches onto Shinji very quickly and even realizes that her attachment to him is very superficial in the wake of Rei's "love" (her grand misinterpretation in the elevator scene) This of course earns her some respect and is really the first time she goes out of her way to make others happy at the cost of some of her own happiness (which is cute and all) I get that 2.0 is very clear about what kind of progress these characters are making and how that all inevitably falls apart. Asuka is set up and developed in a way that makes her fall all the more "tragic" but it feels hollow when it's (as Bardiel was used in the series) just a means of destroying Shinji's relationship with his father and not actually giving Asuka any real relevance at all. (Granted it isn't really important now because Shinji is the one they're developing) I just hope that 3.0 doesn't leave this moment hanging. A few of her character traits themselves are missing in 2.0, they dropped the bad attempts at German and sure that didn't really add anything to begin with but I think they tired old complaint all gels in the pacing itself, we can't develop meaningful bonds with these characters as they are presented in 2.0 and that makes it as a standalone film feel flimsy at best. Oh and I guess she isn't actually "more tsundere" but is just very clearly fixed solely on Shinji this time around making the tsundere aspect all the more apparent. So many of us work with the knowledge that Anno and company are reworking Eva and have to fit that reworking into a series of films but I don't buy the rushed characterization as an overstay of time limitation at all. They didn't have to play up the "plot" or the "mystery" or the "action" in the precise manner that they did leaving husks of character dynamics/characterization in its wake. But of course they did, Rebuild is a spectacle of sorts after all. But as everyone hammered away in the previous thread, 3.0 will make or break Rebuild. It will either deliver on our hopes characterization/character dynamics wise or trod on aimlessly. (and hopefully it doesn't employ shitty visual filters, has better coloring, character animation etc etc) Also it's very hard not to compare this to the original, it is a remake after all. I still firmly believe that if it isn't made to be better than the original that it had no place being developed at all. And I guarantee that Anno knows what tsundere means.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Tue Aug 03, 2010 10:30 pm Lucretius wrote: I don't have a problem with Rebuild Asuka's personality per se; in fact, she pretty much the only relatable human character in a movie populated by Martian nanobots. This isn't a point in the movie's favor, since Shinji is supposed to be the protagonist.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Aug 04, 2010 2:28 am Lucretius wrote: I don't have a problem with Rebuild Asuka's personality per se; in fact, she pretty much the only relatable human character in a movie populated by Martian nanobots. This isn't a point in the movie's favor, since Shinji is supposed to be the protagonist.

[]:             Quote Post by jojojoker » Wed Aug 04, 2010 3:27 am SaltyJoe wrote: I guess her latching on to Shinji was more due to the fact that he was the most convenient and most readily available person to console herself with. If we stick to this interpretation, giving up her own emotional neediness for what she perceives as true love on Rei's part does make her a whole lot more impressing than Shinji with his "Give me my Rei fix, the rest can die." attitude. I wonder whether this will be acknowledged somehow in the next movies.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Wed Aug 04, 2010 4:13 am I think that the changes to Asuka and Shinji were made to make them more fitting for modern time. The original Shinji was made as a reflection of then modern Japan, in a similar way Asuka could be called a reflection of Shinji, their changes were made to fit now modern times. They are still the same people as before with small diferences and a slight name change to make hers and mari's name rhyme with Ayanami, which I'm willing to put money on there been some kind of plot relevant thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Wed Aug 04, 2010 9:17 am As far as the relationship between Asuka/Shinji/Rei I really don't think there is anything really solid in the way of any of them to make a concrete assumption at this point. I mean it's kind of just your typical anime mass understanding of all partys involved. Asuka is the only one with a clear, "Alright I have the hots for this dork." But Rei is just a confused mix of emotions since she doesn't know what emotion is until she meets Shinji. And I think Shinji is as oblivious as ever. We aren't even really sure Shinji likes girls until EoE in the original. I think the same is true here. He is so introverted, I don't think his adolescent brain is even thinking that far in advance. Until I see proof the other way I think he sees Rei as mor eof a sibling than a lover. Asuka sees a male and female interacting and just thinks they are a couple because that is how she is.

[]: Re: Shikinami really isn't so bad             Quote Post by carla » Wed Aug 04, 2010 10:10 am Lonecow12 wrote: He probably just thought, "If I make Asuka a little nicer, then the audience will feel worse when she gets in Unit 03." And no that is not trolling either. That is him doing his job as director/writer.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Aug 04, 2010 10:22 am jojojoker wrote: hmmmm yeah I'm a reixshinji fan but considering the last scene I wasn't sure if Shinji had saved Rei because of his true feelings or he didn't want to lose Rei like Asuka when the task was in his hands.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 04, 2010 11:49 am First, I'm going to say that the name change isn't that bad. There was no need fore theme naming with only two female EVA pilots, but now that they added Mari, it's a nice touch since there are profecies about the eva pilots (which gets a lot more emphasis in Rebuild) It would be strage for 3 of 5 ppl with the same job to have that similar Names, except when destiny itself is involved. Then, I think Shikinami just sounds cooler. Personal taste. Secondly, I didn't think that Rebuild!Asuka was all too different from her Anime-counterpart. Ambitious, with a drive to be special/the best, arrogant and mean, unable to see other people as anything but Rivals. In fact, she's even worse. She's actually PLAYING with her mother's doll, talking to it as if it were her, and she starts to get depressed after the VERY FIRST time she has to work in a team. Yes, she doesn't luv Kaji, but that was never real love but just a symptom of her wanting to be 'special' ("Look at me! I'm so hot, I don't need any of you little boys, I can have an adult BF!") The main difference is: In the original Series, all characters (not just Asuka) stayed passive istead of doing something about their problems, and slowly subcumbed to their issues. Well, everyone except Mari because there was no Mari in the original Series XD Can't imagine Mari Illustrious Nothing-Ventured-Nothing-Gained Makinami being 'passive', and 'not really doing anything about her problems', anyway. In Rebuild, they all try at least, even if there were setbacks. (and heck, Anno didn't make it easier for them. All (returning) Angels took a few levels in badass, not to mention that I don't remember all the oceans being contaminated, any entry plugs getting BITTEN in half or anyone (except Zeruel) getting eaten in the original) Shinji actually gets to do something heroic starting from the end of 1.x, (tough Rebuild Shinji's actions got me to appreciate Original!Shinji's few moments a lot more), Rei starts smiling and using the word 'thank you' a lot more after the first times with Shinji and actually tries to get the two Ikaris to reconcile (I never saw that in any fanfiction or thought of it myself, when it is SO logical that she would do that if she was a bit less passive) , and Asuka, well, Asuka becomes less of a jerk and learns to treat Shinji, Rei and Misato as something else than Rivals. The inner feelings of the Characters weren't changed too much, they just go and act on them/talk about them. You know, I never liked Asuka (much like Lonecow's friends) I appreciated her as a character that absolutely has to be in there, an exaggerated version of a type of person that exists in real life, the envious ppl who define their selfworth by their grades, work, body, partners... everything except their true self and the stuff that is really important for them. She has to be there, as a representation of the 'id', the 'seductress'/'witch' kind of woman etc, but she was violent, a jerk, envious and only thought of herself. She was mean to Shinji because she didn't want to accept the fact that she simply emotionally bonded with him after living with him, or that she had a crush on him (look at Shinji - yes, he desperately lacks self esteem. But apart from that, he's thoughtful, nice and has all sorts of talents.) I liked her as a character for a show, as intellectual concept, but if she had been Real, I'd punch her in the face. In Rebuild, she overcomes that. Even more than that, she does something that made me respect her: there ae all these other Anime girls, like Sakura from Naruto, or Orihime from Bleach for example, who fall in love with a guy that either loves someone else or just isn't intrested in them, and those girls get all depressed/jealous about it, they want to be 'useful' and not rely on the boys anymore, but they just can't seem to use the powers they have, move their ass. Original!Asuka was not a stuttering, crying little something, but she wasn't all too different from that. She just turned that frustration into Anger instead of Sadness, but she was dependent of Shinji even tough she didn't want to be and was very jealous of Shinji and Rei (even tough she had other reasons for hating them.) And what does Asuka do after she has her little not-so-different-Moment with Rei? Easy. She realizes that she doesn't exctly need to POSSES that one Guy to be happy, and becomes a truly independant person. She stops thinking "I have to be the best, so I can't lose that baka to the commander's pet" and suddenly, she doesn't have to feel like she was pwned when the two of them are together (contrast the train scene from episode 22') Like she said in the phose call to Misato, she used to not like to see ppl happy, but she has learned that being around them can be worthwile. When she returns in 3.0, she will return to kick ass. To resume this long post, I think that "Shikinami Asuka Langley" is not meant to replace or be better than "Soryu Asuka Langley". It's just another way for Anno to get his message across, another road her story could have taken. ClasicEva says "If your life sux, you need to move your ass and take your life into your own hands.", EoE says "Your life will suck even more if you don't move your ass and take your life into your own hands"and Rebuild says "Your life will stop sucking if you move your ass and take your life into your own hands" I thought that putting Asuka in eva 03 was a brilliant move. Why? a) There are people who already saw the original.The Bardiel Incident is supposed to be the point where everything starts going downhill, it's meant to be shocking and nerve wrecking. How exactly would putting Touji in there again shock them? So put Asuka in there. They wouldn't expect that, even better: Many of them came to watch this movie to see Asuka, they're attached to her. Did you notice that there's even a slight focus on Touji before the Bardiel Incident? All so that we old fans would be more shocked when Asuka gets Unit 03. b) There are pople who didn't see the original, but saw 01 and probably went all "awww" at the smile scene. I still recall my first reaction to Asuka: "Oh no! Another female pilot, one of these stereotypical chicks who treat the boys badly and still get them in the end! She'll take Shinji from Rei-chan!" (keep in ming that none of the new watchers would know about the clone thing at this point so that reaction is more likely than you think)Then, plot and character developement happens. Asuka kicks ass, shows that she's NOT just a stereotypical bitch, becomes a better human being...So around the bardiel incident would be the time where the new watcher IS JUST GETTNG ATTACHED TO Asuka, perhaps thinking "Uh, she'll be my new fav char, she's so cool! And oh, she can be nice, too!" So now is the most evil moment to rip Asuka from her newfound fans ->increased shock value. Plus, who'd expect the new girl they just introduced to get torn to shreds? (Technicaly, Mari is newer, but the new ppl would have seen her BEFORE Asuka, and no one would have been sad over Mari's death before her fight with Zeruel or her second meeting with Shinji which both took place AFTER the Bardiel Incident)

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Aug 04, 2010 2:51 pm I think the real issue people have with Shikinami is that, for better or worse, we just don't get much of a taste of her personality. We know she's similar enough to Sohryu, but that she's also different, and we don't know WHY she's different. We have no idea what her backround is so pessimists are shouting that she's out of character. They might be completely right and this is just Shikinami being designed with fan-pleasing in mind or something. But then, it's also entirely possible that her psyche and backround are VERY different in a way we might all find satisfactory.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Aug 04, 2010 3:47 pm I think Aura makes a good point. Although what gets me personally is Asuka having a name change to begin with. I really hope there will be some in-universe explanation in Q or Final, because none of the other characters had such a drastic change as part of their name, and if Asuka really IS Anno's favorite, I think he owes it to fans/himself to give her "proper" treatment by explaining the change. I have a theory that Anno intentionally gave Asuka a different last name as a means of being able to differentiate Soryu from Shikinami, that perhaps he didn't WANT Soryu in the new movies so not to "soil" her (I mentioned this here and there was a reply here ), but who knows, I still feel some kind of explanation would be nice. Maybe Asuka changed her last name to distant herself from her mother? Or she took her stepmother's last name? (though the stepmother's German. >_> ), based on the assumption Shikinami DOES have a different past than Soryu. At the very least, I don't see how "They changed her name to fit with the -Nami naming scheme of Shikinami/Ayanami/Makinami" is enough of a reason. Besides that, I personally like Soryu a little bit better than Shikinami, but only because Shikinami's development was somewhat rushed in 2.0 (so I can't say I like them equally despite the differences between them) ; we only got the bare-bones of her personality: Shikinami initially hated Rei for being doll-like YET has a doll hand-puppet (from her mother??), she plays video games just like Asuka would have in Proposal , and also has a hatred of boys like in Proposal/NGE though she warms up to Shinji and gets a crush on him (her feelings for Shinji are somewhat more open than NGE, considering instead of denying her feelings, she manages to have a conversation with Rei about Shinji!), she's still gung-ho about piloting, barely seems to know Kaji this time around.... And yet, there's not much reasoning besides getting JUST the bare-bones of Shikinami. I'm assuming that Q will treat us with either a flashback to the Bardiel mind-rape(?) or a general flashback to Asuka's past in order to flesh her out more.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 04, 2010 4:16 pm Well, her past will surely be revealed sometime in Q or final because, as I said, she is still there, so they must have plot for her. I doubt Bardiel mindraped her, they've been reducing the inner-mindfuck scenes, we'll probably get a train scene with some bits of the dialogue before she wakes up at most. She still has the Doll which means that some version of her background story must have happened. I think she has a hate/love relationship with that Doll or maybe it was originally hers before her mother went mad, or her playing with is was intended to act further creepyness factor. I don't think they changed her past too much, tough. Because why? Rebuild is about OVERCOMING problems, and how can she do that if she has none? As for the 'the different Name is there so Soryu doesn't get 'soiled'' theory, I think there's no point to it, since Anno intended Rebuild as 'improved' Eva. He didn't rename Shinji and Shinji went through way more changes. I've read some early text where Anno announced NGE, saying that he would tell a dark story in a hopeless, post apocalyptic world with characters that can hardly be called 'heroes' and that he wanted that world to improve in the end. Perhaps he went back to that original concept, who knows. The 'Nami' thema Naming MIGHT be a 'sufficient reason' for Anno, we can't rule that out. I mean, why can't her name just be different? I mean, perhaps he thought 'Soryu' was the most fitting name he could come up with, but came to the conclusion that 'Shikinami' just sounds better? Fifteen years passed in the interim. I've once written a little Story where I named the main Character's Rival "Rosetta". Now, that the story is about to be published, I regret that I didn't call her "Rosaline." which I think would've been th better option after some months of thinking. Of course, I'm a dumb kid and by far not a genius like Anno, but I too think that 'Shikinami' has a cooler ring to it... Just personal taste, tough. Why must there be a complicated reason? why can't he just decide to change the characters non-plot-relevant second last name we only heard about thrice in the original Series? I mean, in Rebuild, Shinji calls her 'Shikinami' for a while, but we only hear the 'Soryu' when she introduces herself to Shinji & co, and later the whole class, and then, when those security guys find her. (The few times in the mind rape are technically a flashback) 'Soryu' has no plot relevant meaning. So why can't they just change it for aesthetic reasons or for the theme naming?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Aug 04, 2010 4:18 pm 2.0 attempts to have Asuka be both the evil interloper in a quasi-love-triangle (even if Rei doesn't see it as such, it's clearly presented as one), to be gotten out of the way, and as someone Shinji is supposed to feel bad about having to maim. It's not really possible to do both. If she's supposed to be close enough to Shinji for Bardiel to work as "Shinji has to hurt his friend" they don't have nearly enough of a friendship developed. On the flip side, if it's just supposed to be "Shinji has to hurt someone," and they're not supposed to have been close at all, they have way too much screentime together, and she shouldn't have been sent to live with Misato in the first place. It makes no narrative sense as-is, either way you want to interpret it. Also, the 'theme naming' of 2.0's three female pilots' surnames is silly and commits unspeakable acts to my suspension of disbelief. Even moreso if it's explained with some sort of 'all the girls are clones' story as has been theorized, because of all the new holes and absurdities that would be brought up with it.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 04, 2010 4:37 pm I think the theme naming is very fitting - after all, the Children's fates were predestined and the prophecy thing is accentuated a lot in rebuild. Asuka isn't exactly used as 'evil' interloper, and the fact that she gets killed right after she allowed Rei and Shinji to have their family party is enough to make you care for her, in my opinion. I mean, she just became a good person and someone Shinji could confide in about his feelings. They were on first Name Basis which only close friends and family members do in Japan. I mean, Shinji even calls Rei by her last name and she's like, his love intrest. (Tough that is probably because of him being to shy to ask her if he can call her Rei) At this point, Asuka was pretty much Shinji's best friend, and even if she wasn't, he owed her one for the party thing.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Aug 04, 2010 4:46 pm Kendrix wrote: I think the theme naming is very fitting - after all, the Children's fates were predestined and the prophecy thing is accentuated a lot in rebuild. Asuka isn't exactly used as 'evil' interloper, and the fact that she gets killed right after she allowed Rei and Shinji to have their family party is enough to make you care for her, in my opinion. I mean, she just became a good person and someone Shinji could confide in about his feelings. They were on first Name Basis which only close friends and family members do in Japan. I mean, Shinji even calls Rei by her last name and she's like, his love intrest. (Tough that is probably because of him being to shy to ask her if he can call her Rei) At this point, Asuka was pretty much Shinji's best friend, and even if she wasn't, he owed her one for the party thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 04, 2010 5:08 pm Misato trying to hold Shinji back doesn't mean Asuka was forgotten. IT's one of these 'the characters try at least' things found throuout Rebuild. Misato seems disapointed that Shinji 'won't ask after Rei and Asuka' and we are shown Asuka's empty room and her broken doll in a quarantine-plastic-thingie. Gendo tells Shinji that 'Sacrifices are necessary'. With that, he obviously means Asuka, but don't expect him to use her name, oh no, that would make him sound like... like a dad trying to console his kid. After that, Zeruel arrives and there's not much time to have the characters moping over Asuka - it would be ineffectual anyway as the audience already knows that she survived.

[]: Re: Shikinami really isn't so bad             Quote Post by Lucretius » Wed Aug 04, 2010 6:50 pm carla wrote: cue people begging for a name change because Rei "isn't Rei anymore."

Article: Shikinami really isnt so bad
[]: Shikinami really isn't so bad             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Tue Aug 03, 2010 8:02 pm Disclaimer: I am in no way saying Shikinami is in any way shape or form better than Soryu. The original Asuka is the love of my life (next to my wife) and is probably my favorite character of any work of fiction ever. I know we had a thread like this in the past and it got locked (my fault if I recall), but after I watched 2.22 again, I have to say Asuka Shikinami isn't a bad character at all, she just got a bad wrap from fans. I say this because when I watched Eva 2.22 this time with two friends who hated Asuka before, they now love her. The problem was that as soon as reviews came in for 2.0, everyone was saying, "Asuka is now officially Tsundere." Which may or may not be true, but if you strip away all the bullshit jargon fans have come up with over the years to describe things is anime ("like the director is Trolling us!") you will realize that Asuka is now a little more sweeter than she was before because the audience had to feel bad for her a lot quicker. Lets be honest. If Soryu had been in 2.0, most casual people wouldn't give a shit that she got in Unit 03 and got creamed. They would say, "I hope the bitch is dead." We all like Soryu because we got to see her grow and develop over a longer period of time. Even her sweet moments were filled with hostility. If this film had to succeed for a more casual audience then Asuka had to be a lot more sympathetic, and by all accounts she was. My two friends I mentioned earlier who would give me endless amouts of shit every time I said Asuka Soryu is the greatest character in modern anime, actually felt sad for Shikinami after what happened for her. They felt nothing for Soryu during End of Evangelion. So again, I'm not saying she's better, or worse, she is just different, and she performs her job well within the context of the movie. Anno didn't go out and say, "I'm going to make her more Tsundere." In fact I'd be surprised if he even is aware of the term. A

Article: Shikinami really isnt so bad
 and she performs her job well within the context of the movie. Anno didn't go out and say, "I'm going to make her more Tsundere." In fact I'd be surprised if he even is aware of the term. A lot of these directors don't follow fan lingo or theorys at all and he seems like the kind of guy who could give a crap. (Yes I know Gainax is an Otaku studio that he started, but he is far far removed from the kid he was when he started Gainax). He probably just thought, "If I make Asuka a little nicer, then the audience will feel worse when she gets in Unit 03." And no that is not trolling either. That is him doing his job as director/writer. I think as fans we over think some things. From a strictly storyline standpoint, Asuka Shikinami is a good character. Standing next to Soryu, she doesn't compare, but that isn't what she is meant to do.

 clearly and deliberately play on fan expectations and flip them on you. The idea of surprising the old audience had them thinking what they presented in 2.0 was crushing and emotionally distressing but I don't think that it resonates any more clearly than what we got in the series. Asuka latches onto Shinji very quickly and even realizes that her attachment to him is very superficial in the wake of Rei's "love" (her grand misinterpretation in the elevator scene) This of course earns her some respect and is really the first time she goes out of her way to make others happy at the cost of some of her own happiness (which is cute and all) I get that 2.0 is very clear about what kind of progress these characters are making and how that all inevitably falls apart. Asuka is set up and developed in a way that makes her fall all the more "tragic" but it feels hollow when it's (as Bardiel was used in the series) just a means of destroying Shinji's relationship with his father and not actually giving Asuka any real relevance at all. (Granted it isn't really important now because Shinji is the one they're developing) I just hope that 3.0 doesn't leave this moment hanging. A few of her character traits themselves are missing in 2.0, they dropped the bad attempts at German and sure that didn't really add anything to begin with but I think they tired old complaint all gels in the pacing itself, we can't develop meaningful bonds with these characters as they are presented in 2.0 and that makes it as a standalone film feel flimsy at best. Oh and I guess she isn't actually "more tsundere" but is just very clearly fixed solely on Shinji this time around making the tsundere aspect all the more apparent. So many of us work with the knowledge that Anno and company are reworking Eva and have to fit that reworking into a series of films but I don't buy the rushed characterization as an overstay of time limitation at all. They didn't have to play up the "plot" or the "mystery" or the "action" in the precise manner that they did leaving husks of character dynamics/characterization in its wake. But of course they did, Rebuild is a spectacle of sorts after all. But as everyone hammered away in the previous thread, 3.0 will make or break Rebuild. It will either deliver on our hopes characterization/

Article: Shikinami really isnt so bad
 in its wake. But of course they did, Rebuild is a spectacle of sorts after all. But as everyone hammered away in the previous thread, 3.0 will make or break Rebuild. It will either deliver on our hopes characterization/character dynamics wise or trod on aimlessly. (and hopefully it doesn't employ shitty visual filters, has better coloring, character animation etc etc) Also it's very hard not to compare this to the original, it is a remake after all. I still firmly believe that if it isn't made to be better than the original that it had no place being developed at all. And I guarantee that Anno knows what tsundere means.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 04, 2010 11:49 am First, I'm going to say that the name change isn't that bad. There was no need fore theme naming with only two female EVA pilots, but now that they added Mari, it's a nice touch since there are profecies about the eva pilots (which gets a lot more emphasis in Rebuild) It would be strage for 3 of 5 ppl with the same job to have that similar Names, except when destiny itself is involved. Then, I think Shikinami just sounds cooler. Personal taste. Secondly, I didn't think that Rebuild!Asuka was all too different from her Anime-counterpart. Ambitious, with a drive to be special/the best, arrogant and mean, unable to see other people as anything but Rivals. In fact, she's even worse. She's actually PLAYING with her mother's doll, talking to it as if it were her, and she starts to get depressed after the VERY FIRST time she has to work in a team. Yes, she doesn't luv Kaji, but that was never real love but just a symptom of her wanting to be 'special' ("Look at me! I'm so hot, I don't need any of you little boys, I can have an adult BF!") The main difference is: In the original Series, all characters (not just Asuka) stayed passive istead of doing something about their problems, and slowly subcumbed to their issues. Well, everyone except Mari because there was no Mari in the original Series XD Can't imagine Mari Illustrious Nothing-Ventured-Nothing-Gained Makinami being 'passive', and 'not really doing anything about her problems', anyway. In Rebuild, they all try at least, even if there were setbacks. (and heck, Anno didn't make it easier for them. All (returning) Angels took a few levels in badass, not to mention that I don't remember all the oceans being contaminated, any entry plugs getting BITTEN in half or anyone (except Zeruel) getting eaten in the

Article: Shikinami really isnt so bad
 them. All (returning) Angels took a few levels in badass, not to mention that I don't remember all the oceans being contaminated, any entry plugs getting BITTEN in half or anyone (except Zeruel) getting eaten in the original) Shinji actually gets to do something heroic starting from the end of 1.x, (tough Rebuild Shinji's actions got me to appreciate Original!Shinji's few moments a lot more), Rei starts smiling and using the word 'thank you' a lot more after the first times with Shinji and actually tries to get the two Ikaris to reconcile (I never saw that in any fanfiction or thought of it myself, when it is SO logical that she would do that if she was a bit less passive) , and Asuka, well, Asuka becomes less of a jerk and learns to treat Shinji, Rei and Misato as something else than Rivals. The inner feelings of the Characters weren't changed too much, they just go and act on them/talk about them. You know, I never liked Asuka (much like Lonecow's friends) I appreciated her as a character that absolutely has to be in there, an exaggerated version of a type of person that exists in real life, the envious ppl who define their selfworth by their grades, work, body, partners... everything except their true self and the stuff that is really important for them. She has to be there, as a representation of the 'id', the 'seductress'/'witch' kind of woman etc, but she was violent, a jerk, envious and only thought of herself. She was mean to Shinji because she didn't want to accept the fact that she simply emotionally bonded with him after living with him, or that she had a crush on him (look at Shinji - yes, he desperately lacks self esteem. But apart from that, he's thoughtful, nice and has all sorts of talents.) I liked her as a character for a show, as intellectual concept, but if she had been Real, I'd punch her in the face. In Rebuild, she overcomes that. Even more than that, she does something that made me respect her: there ae all these other Anime girls, like Sakura from Naruto, or Orihime from Bleach for example, who fall in love with a guy that either loves someone else or just isn't intrested in them, and those girls get all depressed/jealous about it

Article: Shikinami really isnt so bad
 girls, like Sakura from Naruto, or Orihime from Bleach for example, who fall in love with a guy that either loves someone else or just isn't intrested in them, and those girls get all depressed/jealous about it, they want to be 'useful' and not rely on the boys anymore, but they just can't seem to use the powers they have, move their ass. Original!Asuka was not a stuttering, crying little something, but she wasn't all too different from that. She just turned that frustration into Anger instead of Sadness, but she was dependent of Shinji even tough she didn't want to be and was very jealous of Shinji and Rei (even tough she had other reasons for hating them.) And what does Asuka do after she has her little not-so-different-Moment with Rei? Easy. She realizes that she doesn't exctly need to POSSES that one Guy to be happy, and becomes a truly independant person. She stops thinking "I have to be the best, so I can't lose that baka to the commander's pet" and suddenly, she doesn't have to feel like she was pwned when the two of them are together (contrast the train scene from episode 22') Like she said in the phose call to Misato, she used to not like to see ppl happy, but she has learned that being around them can be worthwile. When she returns in 3.0, she will return to kick ass. To resume this long post, I think that "Shikinami Asuka Langley" is not meant to replace or be better than "Soryu Asuka Langley". It's just another way for Anno to get his message across, another road her story could have taken. ClasicEva says "If your life sux, you need to move your ass and take your life into your own hands.", EoE says "Your life will suck even more if you don't move your ass and take your life into your own hands"and Rebuild says "Your life will stop sucking if you move your ass and take your life into your own hands" I thought that putting Asuka in eva 03 was a brilliant move. Why? a) There are people who already saw the original.The Bardiel Incident is supposed to be the point where everything starts going downhill, it's meant to be shocking and nerve wrecking. How exactly would putting

Article: Shikinami really isnt so bad
 03 was a brilliant move. Why? a) There are people who already saw the original.The Bardiel Incident is supposed to be the point where everything starts going downhill, it's meant to be shocking and nerve wrecking. How exactly would putting Touji in there again shock them? So put Asuka in there. They wouldn't expect that, even better: Many of them came to watch this movie to see Asuka, they're attached to her. Did you notice that there's even a slight focus on Touji before the Bardiel Incident? All so that we old fans would be more shocked when Asuka gets Unit 03. b) There are pople who didn't see the original, but saw 01 and probably went all "awww" at the smile scene. I still recall my first reaction to Asuka: "Oh no! Another female pilot, one of these stereotypical chicks who treat the boys badly and still get them in the end! She'll take Shinji from Rei-chan!" (keep in ming that none of the new watchers would know about the clone thing at this point so that reaction is more likely than you think)Then, plot and character developement happens. Asuka kicks ass, shows that she's NOT just a stereotypical bitch, becomes a better human being...So around the bardiel incident would be the time where the new watcher IS JUST GETTNG ATTACHED TO Asuka, perhaps thinking "Uh, she'll be my new fav char, she's so cool! And oh, she can be nice, too!" So now is the most evil moment to rip Asuka from her newfound fans ->increased shock value. Plus, who'd expect the new girl they just introduced to get torn to shreds? (Technicaly, Mari is newer, but the new ppl would have seen her BEFORE Asuka, and no one would have been sad over Mari's death before her fight with Zeruel or her second meeting with Shinji which both took place AFTER the Bardiel Incident)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Aug 04, 2010 3:47 pm I think Aura makes a good point. Although what gets me personally is Asuka having a name change to begin with. I have a theory that Anno intentionally gave Asuka a different last name as a means of being able to differentiate Soryu from Shikinami, that perhaps he didn't WANT Soryu in the new movies so not to "soil" her (I mentioned this here and there was a reply here ), but who knows, I still feel some kind of explanation would be nice. Maybe Asuka changed her last name to distant herself from her mother? Or she took her stepmother's last name? (though the stepmother's German. >_> ), based on the assumption Shikinami DOES have a different past than Soryu. At the very least, I don't see how "They changed her name to fit with the -Nami naming scheme of Shikinami/Ayanami/Makinami" is enough of a reason. Besides that, I personally like Soryu a little bit better than Shikinami, but only because Shikinami's development was somewhat rushed in 2.0 (so I can't say I like them equally despite the differences between them) ; we only got the bare-bones of her personality: Shikinami initially hated Rei for being doll-like YET has a doll hand-puppet (from her mother??), she plays video games just like Asuka would have in Proposal , and also has a hatred of boys like

Article: Shikinami really isnt so bad
 of her personality: Shikinami initially hated Rei for being doll-like YET has a doll hand-puppet (from her mother??), she plays video games just like Asuka would have in Proposal , and also has a hatred of boys like in Proposal/NGE though she warms up to Shinji and gets a crush on him (her feelings for Shinji are somewhat more open than NGE, considering instead of denying her feelings, she manages to have a conversation with Rei about Shinji!), she's still gung-ho about piloting, barely seems to know Kaji this time around.... And yet, there's not much reasoning besides getting JUST the bare-bones of Shikinami. I'm assuming that Q will treat us with either a flashback to the Bardiel mind-rape(?) or a general flashback to Asuka's past in order to flesh her out more.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 04, 2010 4:16 pm Well, her past will surely be revealed sometime in Q or final because, as I said, she is still there, so they must have plot for her. I doubt Bardiel mindraped her, they've been reducing the inner-mindfuck scenes, we'll probably get a train scene with some bits of the dialogue before she wakes up at most. She still has the Doll which means that some version of her background story must have happened. I think she has a hate/love relationship with that Doll or maybe it was originally hers before her mother went mad, or her playing with is was intended to act further creepyness factor. I don't think they changed her past too much, tough. Because why? Rebuild is about OVERCOMING problems, and how can she do that if she has none? As for the 'the different Name is there so Soryu doesn't get 'soiled'' theory, I think there's no point to it, since Anno intended Rebuild as 'improved' Eva. He didn't rename Shinji and Shinji went through way more changes. I've read some early text where Anno announced NGE, saying that he would tell a dark story in a hopeless, post apocalyptic world with characters that can hardly be called 'heroes' and that he wanted that world to improve in the end. Perhaps he went back to that original concept, who knows. The 'Nami' thema Naming MIGHT be a 'sufficient reason' for

Article: Shikinami really isnt so bad
 that can hardly be called 'heroes' and that he wanted that world to improve in the end. Perhaps he went back to that original concept, who knows. The 'Nami' thema Naming MIGHT be a 'sufficient reason' for Anno, we can't rule that out. I mean, why can't her name just be different? I mean, perhaps he thought 'Soryu' was the most fitting name he could come up with, but came to the conclusion that 'Shikinami' just sounds better? Fifteen years passed in the interim. I've once written a little Story where I named the main Character's Rival "Rosetta". Now, that the story is about to be published, I regret that I didn't call her "Rosaline." which I think would've been th better option after some months of thinking. Of course, I'm a dumb kid and by far not a genius like Anno, but I too think that 'Shikinami' has a cooler ring to it... Just personal taste, tough. Why must there be a complicated reason? why can't he just decide to change the characters non-plot-relevant second last name we only heard about thrice in the original Series? I mean, in Rebuild, Shinji calls her 'Shikinami' for a while, but we only hear the 'Soryu' when she introduces herself to Shinji & co, and later the whole class, and then, when those security guys find her. (The few times in the mind rape are technically a flashback) 'Soryu' has no plot relevant meaning. So why can't they just change it for aesthetic reasons or for the theme naming?

Article: Shikinami really isnt so bad
 enough to Shinji for Bardiel to work as "Shinji has to hurt his friend" they don't have nearly enough of a friendship developed. On the flip side, if it's just supposed to be "Shinji has to hurt someone," and they're not supposed to have been close at all, they have way too much screentime together, and she shouldn't have been sent to live with Misato in the first place. Also, the 'theme naming' of 2.0's three female pilots' surnames is silly and commits unspeakable acts to my suspension of disbelief. Even moreso if it's explained with some sort of 'all the girls are clones' story as has been theorized, because of all the new holes and absurdities that would be brought up with it.
